entry.title: [Duran] UK Burnham is Blair 3.0, UK Gets More of the Same as Britain Sinks
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2556 Britain is getting a more Blairite government than Starmer ever ran, and Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris of The Duran break down exactly what Andy Burnham's rise to prime minister really means: the same plan, the same loyalty to Project Ukraine, and the same drift back toward the EU that has driven Britain's longest decline in living standards since the industrial revolution. With a Blair loyalist installed as chief of staff, West Streeting likely as finance minister, and Burnham quietly signaling a Brexit reversal, the question is not whether things will change, but how much worse they have to get before Britain finally changes course.
00:00:00 Introduction: UK After Starmer 00:00:21 Burnham Transition Delayed to September? 00:01:33 Establishment Factions Resist Change 00:02:04 Burnham Picks Blair Loyalist as Chief of Staff 00:03:00 Starmer Had a Plan and So Does Burnham 00:04:21 Who Becomes Finance Minister? 00:06:27 Burnham Furious Over NATO Meeting and Defense Review 00:07:21 Brexit: Will Burnham Reverse It? 00:11:08 Britain's Economic Reality Since 2008 00:15:10 What a Real Reform Government Could Do

Britain is getting a more Blairite government than Starmer ever ran, and Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris of The Duran break down exactly what Andy Burnham's rise to prime minister really means: the same plan, the same loyalty to Project Ukraine, and the same drift back toward the EU that has driven Britain's longest decline in living standards since the industrial revolution. entry.title: [Duran] Russia Frontline Advance. Putin's Messaging Woes as Lavrov's WW3 Warning Ignored
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2555 Ukraine's front lines are crumbling as Russian forces close in on Konstantinovka, Lyman, and Sumy while the collective West media refuses to report any of it. Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris on The Duran break down how Putin is surrendering the information war to Zelensky by default, why the drone narrative is a smokescreen for catastrophic Ukrainian battlefield losses, and what Lavrov's chilling WWII comparison at the Russian Security Council really means.

entry.title: [Duran] Rutte claims NATO helped attack Iran as Trump pivots to Ukraine
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2553 Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris on The Duran break down the Rutte and Trump meeting ahead of the NATO summit, revealing how the failed Iran war has left both Trump and NATO significantly weakened on the world stage. With 5,000 US air missions flown from European bases and the full NATO alliance still unable to achieve its military objectives against Iran, the uncomfortable truth is that the collective West's greatest military alliance has been exposed as far less powerful than advertised. As a deflated Trump pivots back toward Project Ukraine with renewed pledges of support for Zelensky, is he being maneuvered into exactly the position NATO and the neocons always wanted him in?
00:00:00 Rutte Travels to Meet Trump Ahead of NATO Summit 00:01:22 Trump Diminished After Iran War Failure 00:02:47 America First Agenda Now Dead 00:03:44 What Iran and Ukraine Revealed About NATO Power 00:05:39 Is NATO Spending Theater the Real Goal 00:07:27 The Whole Alliance Was Involved and Still Lost 00:10:33 5,000 US Missions From European Bases 00:13:04 NATO Summit to Push Hard on Project Ukraine 00:17:18 US Returns to Backing Ukraine at UN Security Council 00:20:05 Ukraine Drone Strikes as Performance for Trump

entry.title: [Duran] US-Iran Ceasefire Talks. Conflict Round 3 Is Coming
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2551 Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris of The Duran break down the 18 hours of US-Iran negotiations in Geneva, where JD Vance and Steve Witkoff met with Iranian Foreign Minister Araqchi and Ghalibaf in talks mediated by Pakistan and Qatar, producing a cautious framework agreement while leaving the deepest questions entirely unresolved. With neocon fury building in Washington, a contentious four and a half hour Trump-Graham meeting suggesting major behind-the-scenes pressure, and Iran maintaining its red line on Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz, Alex and Alexander argue that the ceasefire is merely a pause before a far larger confrontation.

entry.title: [Duran] Keir Starmer Resigns as UK Prime Minister. Burnham's coronation awaits
entry.summary:
The Duran: Episode 2550 Keir Starmer has announced his resignation as UK Prime Minister, but in a move that exposes how deeply broken British politics has become, he will remain in office for weeks, possibly all the way through September, leaving Britain effectively without a functioning government. Alex Christoforou and Alexander Mercouris break down why Andy Burnham is now the inevitable successor, why the transition is being deliberately dragged out, and what the deep state is planning to tell Burnham before he ever sets foot in Downing Street. Is Britain about to swap one puppet for another, or is there any chance Burnham breaks from the script?
00:00:00 Starmer Announces Resignation 00:01:25 Why Starmer Lasted So Long Despite Failing 00:03:26 Andy Burnham Wins Makerfield and Changes Everything 00:04:29 Starmers Weekend Collapse and the Numbers That Weren't There 00:05:44 Britain's Broken Political Transition Problem 00:07:03 Weeks of Political Paralysis Ahead 00:08:17 The Burnham Coronation and Why It Was Delayed 00:11:35 Burnham Could Be PM Today But Chose Not to Be 00:12:21 The Deep State Steps In 00:14:48 What Burnham Will Be Told and What He Will Agree To
